risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "with half of the children randomly assigned to a concurrent 14-day course of oral ciprofloxacin ... and half to oral placebo. Children were randomized to one of two maintenance treatment strategies" Comment: The study explicitly states that participants were randomly assigned/randomized, and the context of placebo-controlled treatment and maintenance strategy randomization supports a proper random allocation process. Although the exact randomization mechanism is not described, the wording provides sufficient evidence for low risk under a permissive Cochrane judgment.

_evidence.method_quote: During the first quarter of the study, all children received initial Pa eradication treatment consisting of 28–56 days of open label tobramycin inhalation solution (TIS, Novartis Pharmaceutical Corp), with half of the children randomly assigned to a concurrent 14-day course of oral ciprofloxacin (Bayer Healthcare AG) and half to oral placebo. Children were randomized to one of two maintenance treatment strategies administered over the five remaining quarters (15-months): (1) Cycled therapy: treatment provided in quarterly cycles regardless of findings from scheduled quarterly respiratory cultures, or (2) Culture-based therapy: treatment only in response to identification of Pa from quarterly cultures.
_evidence.method_summary: Children were randomized to concurrent ciprofloxacin vs placebo in the first quarter, and to cycled vs culture-based maintenance therapy over 15 months.
context_clues: Children were randomized to one of two maintenance treatment strategies

risk_of_bias.support_text: Summary: The article states that children were randomized, but it does not describe any mechanism used to conceal the upcoming assignment from the recruiters. The extracted evidence explicitly says the concealment mechanism was "not described" and that no concealment details were reported. Comment: Randomization alone does not establish allocation concealment; without an explicit central system, SNOSE, sequentially numbered identical containers, or another clearly preventing mechanism, the risk of allocation concealment bias remains unclear.

_evidence.note: Methods describe that children were randomized, but the article does not describe how the upcoming assignment was concealed from recruiters.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "During the first quarter of the study, all children received initial Pa eradication treatment consisting of 28–56 days of open label tobramycin inhalation solution (TIS, Novartis Pharmaceutical Corp), with half of the children randomly assigned to a concurrent 14-day course of oral ciprofloxacin (Bayer Healthcare AG) and half to oral placebo. ... Treatment administered during the 15-month maintenance phase in both arms consisted of 28 days open-label TIS with either a concurrent 14-day course of oral ciprofloxacin or placebo as received during the first quarter" Comment: The intervention was explicitly open-label for TIS, and children received ciprofloxacin versus placebo only concurrently with this visible treatment strategy; the article does not indicate successful blinding of participants/personnel. Because the study reports subjective/clinically adjudicated outcomes such as pulmonary exacerbation treated with antibiotics, knowledge of allocation could plausibly influence care-seeking, symptom reporting, or treatment decisions, so performance bias is high overall.

_evidence.blinding_quote: During the first quarter of the study, all children received initial Pa eradication treatment consisting of 28–56 days of open label tobramycin inhalation solution (TIS, Novartis Pharmaceutical Corp), with half of the children randomly assigned to a concurrent 14-day course of oral ciprofloxacin (Bayer Healthcare AG) and half to oral placebo. ... Treatment administered during the 15-month maintenance phase in both arms consisted of 28 days open-label TIS with either a concurrent 14-day course of oral ciprofloxacin or placebo as received during the first quarter
_evidence.blinding_method: open label tobramycin inhalation solution (TIS) with oral ciprofloxacin versus oral placebo

risk_of_bias.support_text: Summary: The study states that "All cultures were processed at the Center for CF Microbiology at Seattle Children’s Hospital" and that "An a priori, established definition of pulmonary exacerbation was used for the determination of these events." The extracted evidence identifies the assessor role as a "blinded assessor." The reported outcomes for this analysis were objective or standardized outcomes (Pa culture status and pulmonary exacerbation defined by a priori criteria), so knowledge of allocation would be unlikely to influence outcome measurement.

outcomes.label: pulmonary exacerbation treated with IV antibiotics
pulmonary exacerbation treated with antibiotics by any route of administration

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "A total of 282/304 (93%) participants in the EPIC clinical trial were included in the study cohort, based on the availability of the initial eradication outcome at week 10. The 22 excluded children were similar to those included in the current analysis except for a slightly younger age and lower BMI." Comment: The paper reports overall exclusion of 22/304 participants and gives the week-10 eradication subgroup sizes (245 and 37), but it does not provide a CONSORT-style per-arm attrition flow for the analysed maintenance outcomes or enough information to determine whether missing outcome data were balanced by randomised arm at the analysed time point. Although the extracted data suggest complete case analysis and an apparent arm difference in loss, the report as provided does not let us verify post-randomisation attrition on the outcome of interest with confidence, so the appropriate judgement is Unclear rather than High.

per_arm_flow.reasons: A total of 282/304 (93%) participants in the EPIC clinical trial were included in the study cohort, based on the availability of the initial eradication outcome at week 10.
There were 245/282 (87%) who achieved successful initial eradication, and 37/282 children (13%) who failed to eradicate recently acquired Pa at week 10.

_evidence.notes: The analysis cohort was restricted to participants with the week 10 initial eradication outcome available (282/304). The paper does not provide a CONSORT-style attrition flow by randomized arm for the analyzed maintenance outcomes.
